top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses how AI agents are emerging as highly privileged identities that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are inadequate to govern. The core argument centers on the limitations of standing privilege models when applied to AI agents, positioning this as a gap requiring new approaches to PAM and identity security.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat actor group tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign using fake Okta login pages combined with social engineering and impersonation of support staff. The attackers use these fraudulent Okta authentication pages to harvest credentials and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems for data theft and extortion.

top_stories.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
top_stories.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft by binding browser sessions to the device. macOS support is planned for a future Chrome release, with DBSC aiming to mitigate token/session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA.

top_stories.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
top_stories.summary: Specops published a piece outlining five ways Zero Trust architecture can maximize identity security. The article covers limiting access, enforcing device trust, and blocking lateral movement as countermeasures against credential theft and privilege escalation.

top_stories.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
top_stories.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses governance and visibility challenges for machine and AI agent identities in a Help Net Security interview. The conversation draws on his experience at IBM and CyberArk to address how AI has driven convergence of machine and AI identity management into a unified problem space.

news.title: Anthropic tests user trust with ID and selfie checks for Claude
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced identity verification for its Claude AI chatbot, requiring users to submit government-issued ID and selfie checks, making it the first major AI chatbot platform to implement such verification. This development touches on identity proofing and user authentication practices in consumer-facing AI services, with no equivalent requirement currently imposed by competitors like OpenAI's ChatGPT or Google's Gemini.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agents—Copilot and Agentforce—that could have allowed external attackers to exfiltrate sensitive data. The flaws were fixed recently and relate to AI agent behavior rather than core identity or access control mechanisms.

news.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
news.summary: Microsoft awarded $2.3 million in bug bounties to security researchers following nearly 700 submissions to its Zero Day Quest hacking contest. The contest focused on cloud and AI security vulnerabilities in Microsoft's platform.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a major patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

news.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
news.summary: Over 100 malicious Chrome Web Store extensions have been discovered stealing Google OAuth2 Bearer tokens and deploying backdoors. The attack targets authentication tokens used in OAuth2 flows, potentially allowing attackers to hijack user accounts without credentials.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring multi-factor authentication capabilities to web applications. The product targets MFA delivery through the browser layer rather than application-level integration.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity security capabilities as part of a broader AI infrastructure strategy. The acquisition positions Cisco to manage non-human identities and machine-to-machine access in agentic AI environments.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is an open-source tool (Rust, Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the host through PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to cryptographically bind tokens to the requesting party, distinguishing it from other OIDC-for-SSH approaches.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a new backdoor designed to harvest credentials from major cloud environments including AWS, Google Azure, and Alibaba. The campaign uses typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communication and achieves near-zero detection rates.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and seizing associated infrastructure. The operation targeted account credentials at scale, with W3LL functioning as a phishing-as-a-service toolkit capable of bypassing MFA and enabling over $20 million in fraud attempts.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, a security feature that uses hardware keys to prevent infostealers from exploiting stolen session cookies on Windows. DBSC cryptographically binds authenticated sessions to a specific device, making stolen cookies unusable for session hijacking attacks.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt in response. The focus is on identifying malicious activity that mimics legitimate user behavior, a core challenge in identity and access management.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where infostealer malware extracts and exfiltrates authentication cookies. The feature cryptographically binds session credentials to a specific device, preventing stolen cookies from being used on attacker-controlled systems.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group continues conducting global cyberattacks, with security experts recommending patching and zero trust adoption as essential defensive measures. The article frames zero trust as a non-negotiable response to nation-state level threats rather than detailing specific IAM implementations or products.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise authentication credentials at scale.

news.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
news.summary: The article examines how AI agents with broad access to computers, files, and online services are shifting security priorities for organizations. The piece addresses how these tools blur lines around trust, insider threats, and access control, touching on IAM-relevant concepts around authorization and access management for autonomous agents.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes between the target and legitimate sites. The service bypasses traditional anti-phishing takedowns by using the actual brand's website rather than static copies, effectively defeating MFA protections.
